Text:
Good news for rural healthcare in Zimbabwe! Karanda Hospital is stepping up.

The hospital is expanding its services to meet the growing demand in rural communities. This move is crucial as access to quality healthcare remains a significant challenge for many Zimbabweans outside major urban centers. It signals a positive step towards addressing health disparities and ensuring more people get the care they need.

This expansion means more families will have better access to essential medical services closer to home, potentially saving lives and improving overall community well-being. A true lifeline for many. 🏥

Text:
A crucial new phase for education and empowerment just launched in Zimbabwe.

The O3 PLUS Programme, an initiative focused on empowering young people through education and health, has officially launched its second phase, set to run from 2026 to 2029. This continuation signifies a renewed commitment to addressing critical issues faced by students in higher education institutions across the country.

This long-term program aims to foster safer, healthier, and more inclusive learning environments, directly impacting the lives of countless young Zimbabweans. It's about building a brighter future, one student at a time.

Full briefing — link in bio.
via UNESCO
